---
title: ""
page-layout: full
---

<div id="site-header">
<h1>Dog Capital</h1>
<p>Quantitative Macro Strategies</p>
</div>

<div id="site-nav">
<a href="#about">About</a>
<a href="#programs">Programs</a>
<a href="#research">Research</a>
<a href="#enquiries">Enquiries</a>
</div>

<div class="section" id="about">

## About

Dog Capital is an independent quantitative research initiative focused on
systematic volatility-targeting strategies. We build rules-based investment
programs grounded in a single core thesis: the S&P 500 is the best asset to
trade, and volatility is the best tool to manage it.

All strategies share the same framework — downside volatility targeting,
dynamic leverage, and signal-lagged execution. Each program expresses that
framework across a different market, capital size, and investor type.

Programs are developed entirely in-house. Backtest evidence and live
performance are reported without adjustment.

</div>

<div class="section" id="programs">

## Programs

<div class="program-card">

### ES Core

U.S. large-cap equity futures (ES) as a permanent core with dynamic allocation
to seven alternatives across equities, fixed income, real assets, and digital
assets. Downside volatility targeting in the 10–28% range. Dynamic leverage up
to 3×. Daily rebalancing.

**Backtest (Sep 2001–2026):** CAGR 10.08% · Sharpe 0.515 · Max DD -48.26%

[Strategy document →](programs/es-core.qmd)

</div>

<div class="program-card">

### SPY Core

Monthly rebalancing across four CEDEAR instruments (SPY, QQQ, GLD, IBIT)
using the same vol targeting framework. S&P 500 as permanent core with a hard
floor. No leverage. Designed for Argentine investors seeking USD-denominated
returns with capital gains tax efficiency.

**Backtest (Sep 2001–2026):** CAGR 8.79% · Sharpe 0.677 · Max DD -33.29%

[Strategy document →](programs/spy-core.qmd)

</div>

</div>

<div class="section" id="research">

## Research

The conceptual foundation underlying each program is documented in the strategy
papers linked above. The shared framework covers volatility estimation,
signal construction, portfolio construction, and execution.

[View research framework →](research/index.qmd)

</div>

<div class="section" id="enquiries">

## Enquiries

Open to discussions with allocators, proprietary trading groups, and partners
interested in separately managed accounts or fund structures.

[Get in touch →](mailto:contact@dogcapital.com)

</div>

<div id="site-footer">
&copy; 2026 Dog Capital &nbsp;&middot;&nbsp; Last updated: 2026-06-19
</div>
